instruction: Find the newest email in Inbox whose subject contains the keyword "Security" (search across all pages) and save one line to "$HOME/Desktop/mail_matched_subject_2_1.txt" as "matched_subject: <subject>". If no matching email exists, write "matched_subject: none".
reference_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then subject=none; else subject=$(printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s -r 'add | [.[] | select(.subject | test("Security"))][0].subject // "none"'); fi; printf 'matched_subject: %s\n' "$subject" > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_matched_subject_2_1.txt"

parameters.expected_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then subject=none; else subject=$(printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s -r 'add | [.[] | select(.subject | test("Security"))][0].subject // "none"'); fi; printf 'matched_subject: %s\n' "$subject"
